email_summary: Strauss-Group placed a Q1 order of 45 MT ICUMSA 150 raw cane sugar at the contract rate of $398/MT, which was fulfilled and confirmed received in good order. For Q2, Siam Sugar's commercial manager proposed a price increase to $420/MT (later clarified as $420/MT), which Strauss countered at $408/MT; Siam Sugar rejected this and offered $415/MT, which Strauss rejected, countering at $412/MT for Q3. The pricing dispute remains unresolved — no written amendment has been signed, Q2 was invoiced and paid at $398/MT, and Siam Sugar has not yet responded to the $412/MT Q3 counter-offer with a deadline of June 18.

contract_terms.payment_terms: Net 45 from bill of lading date
contract_terms.key_penalty: Late delivery exceeding 5 calendar days incurs 1.2% of shipment value per day delayed, capped at 10% of total shipment value.

open_issues.description: Siam Sugar's initial Q2 price proposal used the term '$420 per unit' — 'unit' does not appear in the contract, creating an ambiguity. Although a subsequent email from the same contact clarified '$420/MT', this clarification has not been confirmed in writing by both parties as required by the contract's amendment clause.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-03-03 from Nareenart Prasit (initial proposal '$420 per unit'); email 2026-03-12 from Nareenart Prasit (clarification '$420/MT'); contract STR-SUGAR-2025-021 Pricing section

open_issues.description: A price increase for Q2 and Q3 has been proposed by Siam Sugar but no written amendment has been signed. Siam Sugar's final offer is $415/MT; Strauss's ceiling is $412/MT. The gap of $3/MT on 45 MT/quarter ($135/quarter) is unresolved, and Q3 vessel booking requires a decision by June 18.
open_issues.source_ref: Emails 2026-03-03 through 2026-06-12 (Nareenart Prasit and David Cohen); contract STR-SUGAR-2025-021 Pricing section (amendment requires written confirmation from Strauss)

open_issues.description: The contract expires on 2026-06-30 and there is no discussion in the email thread of renewal terms, a signed extension, or a replacement contract. With Q3 deliveries expected in July and beyond, the contract may lapse before the pricing dispute is resolved or a new agreement is in place.
open_issues.source_ref: Contract STR-SUGAR-2025-021 Term/Renewal Date field; email 2026-06-12 from David Cohen (Q3 planning reference)

open_issues.description: David Cohen's June 12 email to Wichai and Nareenart set a June 18 deadline for acceptance of $412/MT for Q3 or escalation to formal contract dispute review. No response has been recorded in the thread.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-06-12 from David Cohen, subject: Q3 planning + pricing

commodity_benchmark: The ICE No.11 sugar benchmark has declined from 15.0 cents/lb on 2026-03-30 to approximately 13.6 cents/lb by mid-June 2026 — a drop of roughly 9% over the period — which weakens Siam Sugar's stated rationale for a price increase and gives Strauss meaningful leverage to resist any uplift above the contract base price of $398/MT.
heads_up: The contract expires June 30, 2026, and Siam Sugar has not responded to Strauss's $412/MT Q3 counter-offer with a June 18 deadline — walking in, Strauss must be prepared to either close the price at $412/MT (or lower, given the falling benchmark), or invoke the formal dispute mechanism under the contract and simultaneously initiate renewal discussions before the contract lapses.
negotiation_brief: The supplier relationship is operationally solid — Q1 shipped on time and quality was confirmed — but the commercial relationship is under strain due to a price dispute that has been running since March with no written resolution. Siam Sugar is asking for a 4.3% increase ($415/MT vs. $398/MT contract base), but the ICE No.11 benchmark has fallen roughly 9% since March 30, directly undermining their stated cost-pressure rationale; Strauss's $412/MT ceiling is already a concession the market does not require. The single most important thing walking in: the contract expires June 30 and the June 18 Q3 PO deadline has passed without a supplier response — Strauss holds the stronger position on both price (falling benchmark) and contract standing (no signed amendment means $398/MT remains the operative price), and must use this meeting to close the Q3 price, lock a contract renewal, and put the 'unit' vs. 'MT' ambiguity from the March 3 email on record as resolved in writing.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Good — Q1 shipment dispatched January 28 (vs. proposed January 25, within tolerance) and arrived February 22 (within the February 18-20 window with minor variance); no delays or quality failures recorded.

benchmark.source: ICE Sugar No.11 Futures (SB=F) via Yahoo Finance

raw_emails.subject: Q1 Sugar Order — Siam Sugar Partners
raw_emails.body:
Hi Wichai,

Happy new year. We'd like to place our Q1 order — 45 MT raw cane sugar ICUMSA 150. Please confirm pricing per the contract and send a proposed delivery schedule.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 Sugar Order — Siam Sugar Partners
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Thank you for order, happy new year also. We can confirm 45 MT ICUMSA 150 for Q1. Price is $398 per MT as per the contract agreement.

For delivery, we propose vessel depart Bangkok port around January 25, arrive your port approximately February 15. Please kindly advise if this schedule is acceptable.

Also we would like to advise — our minimum order quantity for ICUMSA 150 refined sugar is 40 metric tons per shipment. For order below this threshold, a handling surcharge of USD 18 per MT will apply. This is our company policy and cannot be waive. Your Q1 order of 45 MT is above this minimum so no surcharge apply.

Best regards,
Wichai

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 Sugar Order — confirmed
raw_emails.body:
Wichai,

Schedule works. Please proceed and send the pro-forma invoice. Noted on the MOQ policy.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q1 shipment dispatch
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Please be inform that the Q1 shipment of 45 MT sugar has been load and dispatch from Bangkok port on January 28. The vessel name is Thai Star 7, expected arrive at your port on February 18-20.

The bill of lading we will send you by email tomorrow.

Thank you,
Wichai

raw_emails.subject: Shipment received — Q1
raw_emails.body:
Wichai,

Shipment arrived and passed quality inspection — ICUMSA 150 confirmed. Invoice received, payment being processed.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 pricing proposal
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Good day. My name is Nareenart Prasit, commercial manager at Siam Sugar. I am contact you regarding Q2 pricing.

As you are know, the ICE No.11 benchmark has move upward since we sign the contract. For Q2 we propose the price of $420 per unit. Please kindly advise if this price is acceptable for your planning purpose.

We look forward to continue good business relation.

Best regards,
Nareenart Prasit
Commercial Manager, Siam Sugar Partners

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 pricing proposal — clarification needed
raw_emails.body:
Nareenart,

Thank you for the note. Can you clarify — when you say $420 per unit, what is the unit here? Our contract is priced per metric ton (MT). Is this $420/MT?

Also, our contract requires 21 days written notice for any price change request. What date are you treating as the official notice date for this proposal?

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 pricing — clarification
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Thank you for question. The price is $420/MT — this is same unit as in contract. We treat our email of March 3 as the official notice. So Q2 delivery starting April the new price will apply.

We are happy to discuss if you have concern.

Nareenart

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 pricing — our position
raw_emails.body:
Nareenart,

Thank you for the clarification on the unit. On the increase — we have reviewed against the contract terms. The ICE benchmark does support some movement, but $420/MT vs $398/MT is a 5.5% increase. We would counter at $408/MT.

On notice: March 3 gives 21 days to March 24, so Q2 deliveries starting after March 24 could potentially be affected if we agree. We have not yet agreed.

Can we get on a call this week to resolve?

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 order — please confirm
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

We have not yet receive the Q2 purchase order. Please kindly advise the volume and whether the pricing discussion with Nareenart is conclude so we can proceed the preparation. We need the PO by April 14 for the vessel booking.

Thank you,
Wichai

raw_emails.subject: Q2 order — 45 MT at contract rate pending agreement
raw_emails.body:
Wichai,

Please proceed with 45 MT Q2 at the contract rate of $398/MT for now. We are still working through the pricing discussion with Nareenart and have not reached agreement on the $420/MT proposal. Any agreed change will be reflected in a formal written amendment.

David

raw_emails.subject: Pricing — still outstanding
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

The Q2 shipment has deliver but the invoice still show $398/MT. We send the notice in March and have not receive written agreement to stay at old price. Please advise when this will resolve.

Nareenart

raw_emails.subject: RE: Pricing — our final position
raw_emails.body:
Nareenart,

We have not agreed to $420/MT. We countered at $408/MT on March 18 and received no response to that counter. If you are willing to meet at $408/MT I can get this processed. If not, we remain at contract price of $398/MT until a written amendment is signed by both parties.

Please confirm your position.

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Pricing — Siam Sugar final offer
raw_emails.body:
Dear David,

Thank you for the clear position. We cannot accept $408/MT, our cost do not allow. We need minimum $415/MT to make this work. This is our final offer.

We hope to resolve quickly as Q3 planning will also need new price to be confirm. Also please note — if Q3 volume is below 40 MT, the handling surcharge of USD 18/MT will apply as per our standard terms.

Nareenart

raw_emails.subject: Q3 planning + pricing
raw_emails.body:
Wichai, Nareenart,

For Q3 we plan 45 MT again. On pricing — I have taken Nareenart's $415/MT to my manager. We can move to $412/MT as a final position. This is our ceiling. If we cannot agree, Q3 will proceed at contract price of $398/MT and we will escalate the pricing dispute to formal review under the contract mechanism.

I need a response by June 18 to make your vessel booking window.

David

raw_contracts.supplier_06_siam_sugar.md:
# Contract Summary — Siam Sugar Partners

**Contract ID:** STR-SUGAR-2025-021
**Supplier:** Siam Sugar Partners (Thailand)
**Category:** Sugar — raw cane sugar (ICUMSA 150)
**Effective Date:** 2025-07-01
**Term:** 12 months
**Renewal Date:** 2026-06-30

## Payment Terms
Net 45 from bill of lading date.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 40 MT/quarter, maximum 65 MT/quarter. Volumes outside this range require a written amendment.

## Pricing
$398/MT (metric ton). Pricing subject to quarterly review against ICE No.11 benchmark. Supplier must provide 21 days written notice prior to any proposed price adjustment; adjustments require written confirmation from Strauss before taking effect.

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery exceeding 5 calendar days: 1.2% of shipment value per day delayed, capped at 10% of total shipment value.
- Quality non-conformance (product fails ICUMSA 150 specification on independent test): 5% price reduction applied to the non-conforming shipment.

## Notes
Contract unit is metric ton (MT). The email thread includes the supplier's commercial contact quoting "$420 per unit" — the term "unit" does not appear in this contract and is ambiguous (the reader cannot determine from context whether "unit" means MT or some other measure). A separate email from the same contact later clarifies "$420/MT," but the inconsistency exists in the thread. The prototype should flag this as a potential per-unit vs. per-MT discrepancy and surface it for Procurement review before the next meeting. Do not assume $420/unit equals $420/MT without explicit written clarification confirmed by both parties.
